  • No explicit Capability Lineage provided between AWR and RCS
  • Ambiguous Capabilities:
  • Duplicate items for Knowledge Graph (aw-ideas-to-life-cas-2026-06-06-002 and aw-ideas-to-life-cas-20260605-10)
  • Duplicate items for Business Intent (aw-ideas-to-life-cas-2026-06-06-003 and aw-ideas-to-life-cas-2026-05-31-006)
  • Observations:
  • The registry contains redundant entries for the same architectural intent originating from different workflows (Discovery vs. Vision).
  • Registry status 'done' for several items conflicts with the empty Runtime Capability Snapshot.

docs.title: Strategic Recommendations
docs.text:
• [Governance Data Quality - Critical]: Populate the Runtime Capability Snapshot
  • Rationale: Without implementation evidence, the governance assessment cannot verify architectural alignment.

  • Suggested Action: Run a capability discovery workflow to populate the runtime snapshot.

  • [Architecture Work Quality - High]: Decompose Oversized Work Items

  • Rationale: Items like aw-ideas-to-life-cas-20260614-1 are too broad for effective lifecycle tracking.

  • Suggested Action: Split broad evolution items into specific capability-based Architecture Work.

  • [Governance Lifecycle - Medium]: Consolidate Duplicate Work Items

  • Rationale: Redundant tracking of the same intent (e.g., Knowledge Graph) leads to fragmented evidence.
  • Suggested Action: Merge Discovery and Vision items that share the same architectural outcome.

• Overall Confidence: 0.1
  • Overall Assessment: The governance system is in a state of 'Evidence Disconnect'. While the Architecture Work Registry is highly active and contains detailed planning and status updates, the Runtime Capability Snapshot is empty. This prevents any objective verification of implementation progress.
  • Strengths:
  • Highly detailed Architecture Work Registry with clear initiatives and rationale.
  • Strong use of related_work for traceability within the registry.
  • Clear distinction between discovery, vision, and conversation sources.
  • Risks:
  • Critical gap between planning (AWR) and implementation evidence (RCS).
  • Duplication of architectural intent across multiple AW items (e.g., Knowledge Graph, Business Intent).
  • High volume of 'Proposed' work items relative to 'Active' or 'Done' items.

docs.title: Strategic Recommendations
docs.text:
• [Governance Data Quality - Critical]: Populate the Runtime Capability Snapshot.
  • Rationale: The assessment is currently blocked by a lack of implementation evidence. The snapshot must reflect the current state of the platform to enable governance verification.

  • Suggested Action: Run a capability discovery process to extract current runtime capabilities into the snapshot.

  • [Architecture Work Quality - High]: Deduplicate Architecture Work items.

  • Rationale: Multiple items from different sources (Discovery vs Vision) describe the same architectural goals, leading to fragmented tracking.

  • Suggested Action: Merge duplicate items and use 'related_work' to maintain lineage to original sources.

  • [Governance Lifecycle - Medium]: Decompose oversized work items.

  • Rationale: Large items like 'Continuous Architecture Governance Evolution' mask granular progress and risks.
  • Suggested Action: Break down aw-ideas-to-life-cas-20260614-1 into specific capability-aligned work items.

docs.title: Governance Data Quality
docs.text:
• Missing Lineage:
  • Explicit lineage between AW items and Runtime Capabilities is missing due to empty snapshot.
  • Observations:
  • The Runtime Capability Snapshot is empty, which is the primary blocker for this assessment.
  • Several AW items are near-exact duplicates of each other.
  • Work items from different sources (Discovery vs Vision) are overlapping without being merged or linked.

docs.title: Strategic Recommendations
docs.text:
• [Governance Data Quality - Critical]: Populate the Runtime Capability Snapshot
  • Rationale: The assessment relies on the snapshot as the authoritative implementation state. Without it, 'done' status cannot be validated.

  • Suggested Action: Run a capability discovery or manual registration of implemented components.

  • [Backlog Management - High]: Deduplicate the Architecture Work Registry

  • Rationale: Multiple items cover the Knowledge Graph, Business Intent, and CI Validation, leading to fragmented tracking.

  • Suggested Action: Merge redundant items and use 'related_work' for lineage.

  • [Architecture Work Quality - Medium]: Decompose Oversized Work Items

  • Rationale: Item aw-ideas-to-life-cas-20260614-1 is too broad for effective lifecycle tracking.
  • Suggested Action: Split into specific capability-focused work items.

docs.title: Architecture Work Assessment Report: ideas-to-life-cas
docs.text: Generated at: 2026-07-08T08:45:08Z

docs.title: Executive Summary
docs.text: Evaluated 29 Architecture Work items against the current capability snapshot. Detected 1 oversized items, 72 orphan capabilities, and 0 obsolete/superseded items.

docs.title: Governance Health
docs.text:
• Overall Confidence: 0.85
  • Overall Assessment: The system is rapidly transitioning from a template-ready state to an operationally governed state. Core orchestration and knowledge provider abstractions are complete. The primary governance risk is the proliferation of overlapping Architecture Work items and the lack of explicit lineage for foundational skills.
  • Strengths:
  • Strong implementation of core runtime abstractions (Runner, Knowledge Provider).
  • Comprehensive set of semantic contracts (schemas) for governance artifacts.
  • Automated CI/CD workflows for portal publishing and governance validation.
  • Risks:
  • Oversized Architecture Work items (e.g., aw-ideas-to-life-cas-20260614-1) mask granular progress.
  • Redundant Architecture Work items for Knowledge Graph and Business Intent.
  • Low explicit lineage between AW and runtime capabilities.

docs.title: Governance Data Quality
docs.text:
• Missing Metadata:
  • aw-ideas-to-life-cas-2026-05-31-001 origin_ref is empty
  • Missing Lineage:
  • Most items rely on LLM similarity rather than explicit lineage IDs
  • Ambiguous Capabilities:
  • cap-orchestration-governance---init---3210552cd219
  • Observations:
  • The registry contains multiple items for the same initiatives (e.g., Knowledge Graph) created at different times.
  • Runtime capabilities for 'governance_projection' skills are not explicitly mapped to AW items.

docs.title: Strategic Recommendations
docs.text:
• [Governance Lifecycle - High]: Decompose oversized Architecture Work items
  • Rationale: aw-ideas-to-life-cas-20260614-1 covers too many domains to track effectively.

  • Suggested Action: Split into domain-specific AW items for Portal, Metrics, and Compliance.

  • [Governance Data Quality - Medium]: Consolidate redundant Architecture Work items

  • Rationale: Multiple AW items exist for Knowledge Graph and Business Intent, leading to fragmented tracking.

  • Suggested Action: Merge aw-ideas-to-life-cas-2026-06-06-002 and aw-ideas-to-life-cas-20260605-10.

  • [Traceability - Medium]: Establish explicit lineage for foundational governance skills

  • Rationale: Foundational skills like 'patterns' and 'rules' are currently orphans in the assessment.
  • Suggested Action: Map foundational skills to the Governance Foundation initiative AW items.

docs.title: Architecture Work Assessment Report: ideas-to-life-cas
docs.text: Generated at: 2026-07-08T20:18:58Z

docs.title: Executive Summary
docs.text: Evaluated 29 Architecture Work items against the current capability snapshot. Detected 1 oversized items, 3 orphan capabilities, and 2 obsolete/superseded items.

docs.title: Governance Health
docs.text:
• Overall Confidence: 0.9
  • Overall Assessment: The CAS platform has successfully transitioned from a template-ready state to an operationally governed state for its core runtime and registry. Governance data quality is high for implemented items but shows redundancy in the backlog.
  • Strengths:
  • Strong alignment between core runtime implementation and Architecture Work intent.
  • Comprehensive semantic contract (JSON schema) coverage for governance artifacts.
  • Successful institutionalization of the Architecture Work Registry.
  • Risks:
  • Backlog redundancy (duplicate items for Knowledge Graph and CI validation).
  • Oversized evolution items may mask specific implementation gaps.
  • Orphan capabilities in telemetry and visualization suggest unrecorded architectural intent.

docs.title: Strategic Recommendations
docs.text:
• [Backlog Management - High]: Consolidate duplicate Architecture Work items.
  • Rationale: Redundancy in the registry creates confusion and potential for conflicting status updates.

  • Suggested Action: Merge aw-ideas-to-life-cas-20260605-10 into aw-ideas-to-life-cas-2026-06-06-002 and retire the duplicate.

  • [Governance Structure - Medium]: Decompose oversized Architecture Work items.

  • Rationale: Large items like 'Continuous Architecture Governance Evolution' are difficult to verify as 'Done'.

  • Suggested Action: Split aw-ideas-to-life-cas-20260614-1 into separate items for Action Framework, Compliance Engine, and Metrics.

  • [Traceability - Medium]: Map orphan capabilities to architectural intent.

  • Rationale: Telemetry and DrawIO generation are implemented but lack corresponding Architecture Work records.
  • Suggested Action: Create new Architecture Work items for Observability/Telemetry and Visualization capabilities.

docs.title: Evidence & Conclusions
docs.text:
• Repository contains a comprehensive set of foundational assets, including operational runbooks, agent guidance files, and extensive CAS skill/prompt definitions.
  • Governance templates (ADR, AEM, AID, AW) are well-defined but lack populated instances in the provided asset list, indicating a pre-execution or bootstrap state.
  • Orchestration flows and alignment engines are structurally ready but require actual architecture work and decision records to mature.
  • Knowledge layer templates enforce strict frontmatter and schema contracts, supporting deterministic derivation.

docs.title: Governance Maturity
docs.text: The project exhibits high foundational maturity with robust operational guidance, skill definitions, and prompt contracts. However, governance maturity is currently in the 'template-ready' phase. While the structural contracts for ADRs, AEMs, and AIDs are explicitly defined, there is an absence of actual governance evidence (populated ADRs, alignment reports, or executed architecture work logs) in the provided context. The system is prepared for governance but requires active execution to transition from design to operational maturity.

docs.title: Identified Gaps & Opportunities
docs.text:
• Absence of populated Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) and Architecture Work logs.
  • No live Architecture Evolution Map (AEM) or Architecture Intent Document (AID) instances present.
  • Governance workflow execution history is missing, making drift detection and alignment validation impossible at this stage.
  • Opportunity to bootstrap the first cycle of architecture work and alignment to establish the initial governance baseline.

docs.title: Initial Architecture Work Candidates
docs.text: Description Rationale Priority Execute initial discovery flow to generate first Architecture Work and Alignment Report. Establishes the first governance evidence artifacts and populates the AEM/AID lifecycle. high Create initial Architecture Decision Record (ADR) for core orchestration pipeline. Captures the foundational decision to use the CAS pipeline as the single source of truth for architecture evolution. high Validate and populate knowledge layer templates with project-specific patterns and rules. Ensures the governance substrate is tailored to the project's actual architectural constraints before scaling. medium Implement automated CI validation for CAS skill enforcement. Prevents architectural drift by ensuring all code changes pass through mandatory CAS skills. medium

docs.title: CAS: Why, What, Who, and How
CAS: Why, What, Who, and How
docs.text:
This visual diagram provides a high-level overview of the Continuous Architecture System (CAS). It breaks down the core value proposition of CAS into four distinct pillars.

flowchart TB
    %% ───────── Styling ─────────
    classDef mainTitle fill:#1e293b,stroke:#334155,stroke-width:2px,color:#fff,font-size:20px,font-weight:bold
    classDef pillStyle fill:#3b82f6,stroke:#2563eb,stroke-width:2px,color:#fff,font-size:16px,font-weight:bold
    classDef item      font-size:13px
    classDef flowHead  fill:#e0e7ff,stroke:#6366f1,stroke-width:1px,color:#1e1b4b,font-size:14px,font-weight:bold
    classDef step      font-size:11px
    classDef govStyle  fill:#059669,stroke:#065f46,stroke-width:1px,color:#fff,font-size:13px,font-style:italic
    classDef promStyle fill:#d97706,stroke:#b45309,stroke-width:1px,color:#fff,font-size:13px,font-weight:bold
    classDef bandStyle fill:#7c3aed,stroke:#6d28d9,stroke-width:1px,color:#fff,font-size:14px,font-weight:bold
    classDef cliItem   font-size:13px

    %% ───────── Header ─────────
    Header["🧠 Continuous Architecture System (CAS) — The Enterprise Architecture Harness Platform"]:::mainTitle

    %% ───────── Top row: 4 pillars ─────────
    Why["❓ WHY · The Problem"]:::pillStyle
    What["💡 WHAT · The Value Proposition"]:::pillStyle
    Who["👥 WHO · The Beneficiaries"]:::pillStyle
    How["⚙️ HOW · Mechanism"]:::pillStyle

    Header --- Why
    Header --- What
    Header --- Who
    Header --- How

    %% ───────── Each pillar fans DOWN into its own vertical column ─────────
    W1["Stop Architecture Drift<br/>Bridge the gap between design and execution"]:::item
    W2["Ensure Traceability<br/>Link business intent directly to code"]:::item
    W3["Prevent Technical Debt<br/>Catch structural deviations early"]:::item

    WH1["Living Knowledge Brain<br/>Queryable, continuously updated"]:::item
    WH2["Decision-Driven Governance<br/>ADRs as single source of truth"]:::item
    WH3["Alignment Engine<br/>Compares intended vs. actual architecture"]:::item
    WH4["Prevent & Detect<br/>Stop bad structure upfront, correct drift"]:::item

    U1["🏢 Executives & CTOs<br/>Visibility, governance, ROI"]:::item
    U2["💼 Business Stakeholders<br/>Implementation matches requirements"]:::item
    U3["🛠️ Engineering Teams<br/>Automated guardrails & guidance"]:::item
    U4["🤖 AI Coding Agents<br/>Deep architectural context"]:::item

    %% ───────── HOW column: flows + CLI in one vertical chain ─────────
    CoreTitle["Core Orchestration Loop"]:::bandStyle
    C1["rules-context-loader"]:::step
    C2["architecture-work-to-evolution-map (AEM)"]:::step
    C3["alignment-engine (AIM)"]:::step
    C4["architecture-intent-generator (AID)"]:::step
    C5["governance-validator (continuous loop only)"]:::govStyle

    DiscTitle["Discovery Flow"]:::bandStyle
    D1["baseline-discovery"]:::step
    D2["rules-context-loader"]:::step
    D3["conversation-to-aw / discovery-to-aw / vision-to-aw"]:::step
    D4["→ AEM → AIM → AID"]:::step

    PatTitle["Pattern Flow"]:::bandStyle
    P1["signal-to-pattern-proposals"]:::step
    P2["pattern-scope-classifier"]:::step
    P3["pattern-promotion-workflow"]:::step
    P4["promotion-decision-capture"]:::step
    P5["promotion-to-decision-records (→ ADRs)"]:::step
    P6["approved-adr-to-pattern"]:::promStyle

    SteerTitle["Steering Projection"]:::bandStyle

docs.title: ❓ WHY: The Problem We Solve
docs.text: Architecture is traditionally static, written once, and quickly forgotten. Over time, the actual system diverges from the original design, leading to architecture decay, technical debt, and a disconnect between business requirements and the implemented code. With Coding Agents, this problem is exacerbated, as AI-generated code can introduce structural issues that are difficult to detect and correct.

docs.title: 💡 WHAT: The Value Proposition
docs.text: CAS is an Architecture Harness Platform. It replaces static documents with a living, continuously derived knowledge system. It prevents bad structure from being created in the first place through CAS Skills, and detects and corrects drift using automated workflows. It constantly measures the system's intended architecture against its actual implementation, with Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) serving as the Single Source of Truth (SSOT). It amplifies reuse and reduces cognitive load by providing a Knowledge Layer that is queryable, continuously updated, and accessible to both humans and AI agents.

docs.title: 👥 WHO: The Beneficiaries
docs.text:
• Execs: De-risks technology investments by guaranteeing alignment between spending (engineering effort) and business goals.
  • Architects & Engineers: Removes the burden of manual documentation while providing automated guardrails.
  • AI Ecosystem: Feeds crucial architectural context to autonomous coding agents, making AI-generated code safer and structurally sound.

docs.title: ⚙️ HOW: The Mechanism
docs.text:
CAS exposes a small set of composable flows, skills, and GitHub Actions. Each flow is a deterministic pipeline of steps that read from the knowledge layer and write back to it.

  • cas CLI is the user-facing surface that invokes those flows and adds lifecycle, observability, governance-state, and steering operations.
  • CI/CD pipelines and GitHub Actions invoke the same flows, enabling automated governance and steering of the system's architecture.

docs.title: E2E Steering Projection & Sync Sequence
docs.text:
This diagram illustrates the end-to-end flow of the CAS Steering Projection workflow, from indexing current system state to generating persona-specific LLM-augmented guidance and applying approved updates.

sequenceDiagram
    participant Host as CI / Developer CLI
    participant Runner as FlowRunner
    participant Step as SteeringProjectionStep
    participant Core as Deterministic Engines
    participant Synth as ArchitectureSynthesizer
    participant LLM as LLM (Reasoning Contract)
    participant Rend as ManagedSectionRenderer
    participant Proj as SteeringPatchProjector
    participant Apply as ManagedSectionApplier

    Host->>Runner: cas run steering-projection-flow
    Runner->>Step: execute()

    Note over Step, Core: Phase 1: Deterministic Extraction
    Step->>Core: build_index() [Indexer]
    Step->>Core: analyze(baseline) [Analyzer]
    Step->>Core: extract(deltas) [Extractor]
    Core-->>Step: current_deltas, capabilities

    Note over Step, LLM: Phase 2: Persona-Driven Synthesis
    Step->>Core: scan() [ArtifactScanner]
    Core-->>Step: artifact_claims (markers)
    Step->>Synth: synthesize(request)

    par Parallel Synthesis (README, AGENTS, Platform)
        Synth->>LLM: reason(synthesis-narrative)
        Synth->>LLM: reason(synthesis-rules)
        Synth->>LLM: reason(synthesis-context)
    end
    LLM-->>Synth: synthesized_markdown (JSON)
    Synth-->>Step: synthesized_content_map

    Note over Step, Proj: Phase 3: Projection Bundling
    Step->>Rend: render(project, deltas, capabilities, synth_content)
    Rend-->>Step: managed_section_proposals
    Step->>Proj: persist(project, index, findings, proposals)
    Proj-->>Step: SteeringProjectionBundle (.md)

    Step-->>Runner: bundle_output
    Runner-->>Host: Done. Projection Persisted.

    Note over Host, Apply: Optional Approval Step

    Host->>Apply: cas steering apply --projection <id>
    Apply->>Apply: plan(proposals) [Validation & Diffs]
    Apply->>Apply: apply(plan) [Atomic Surgical Injection]
    Apply-->>Host: Steering Artifacts Updated

docs.title: Flow Description
docs.text:
1. Extraction: The workflow begins by deterministically indexing the current project state and comparing it against a baseline (e.g., previous projection) to extract architectural deltas and capabilities.
  2. Synthesis: The ArchitectureSynthesizer identifies existing managed markers in steering artifacts. It dispatches concurrent requests to the LLM via the unified reasoning contract, providing persona-specific prompts and current content to ensure high-signal, incremental updates.
  3. Bundling: The ManagedSectionRenderer prioritizes synthesized content over raw data tables. The SteeringPatchProjector then persists a human-readable projection bundle containing all proposed changes.
  4. Application: Once the projection is reviewed (manually or via CI), the ManagedSectionApplier performs a surgical, atomic update, injecting the new content strictly between the <!-- CAS-STEERING --> markers.

docs.title: Patterns Illustrated
docs.text:
• Deterministic Core / Selective Augmentation: Core state extraction is deterministic; LLM is used only to improve guidance signal.
  • Persona-Driven Synthesis: Content is tailored for specific audiences (Humans vs. Agents).
  • Safe Surgical Injection: Updates are limited to managed markers to prevent data loss in human-authored sections.

docs.title: Architecture Intent Documents
docs.text:
Latest State

Showing latest version: aid-2026-07-09 (2026-07-09)

docs.title: Strategic Vision
docs.text:
• What we are building: An integrated Architecture Intelligence Platform enabling a continuous loop between Business Intent, Architecture State, and Code Implementation.
  • Why it exists: To eliminate the 15:1 governance velocity bottleneck by embedding governance directly into operational delivery via ReasoningOps.

docs.title: Project Context
docs.text: primary_context: ideas-to-life-cas aw_count: 29 alignment_findings: 3

docs.title: Current State Alignment
docs.text: The system is in a state of 'Critical Persistence Lag'. Implementation of core primitives (Work Registry, Governance Portal) has outpaced formal decision recording, creating a 22:1 proposal-to-acceptance imbalance.

docs.title: Focus Areas / Roadmap
docs.text:
• Phase 1: Governance Reconciliation (Backfill ADRs for Work Registry and Portal).
  • Phase 2: Governance Triage (Process or prune the 22-item backlog).
  • Phase 3: Business Intent Schema Definition (Establish structural requirements for BRD/Context ingestion).
  • Phase 4: Knowledge Graph Implementation (Transition from document-based to graph-based traceability).
  • Phase 5: Automated Drift Remediation (Implement 'Detect-Analyse-Execute' loop).

docs.title: Gaps & Tensions (Risks)
docs.text:
• Governance Velocity Breach: Intelligence generation speed exceeds human review capacity.
  • Implicit Core Implementation: Critical primitives are 'DONE' in implementation but 'PROPOSED' in governance.
  • Business Intent Disconnect: High strategic priority for intent integration but zero implementation evidence in current AEM.
  • Drift Persistence: Formal ADRs are lagging behind the actual system state, leading to 'un-governed' stability.

docs.title: Principles
docs.text:
• Decisions are Central: No capability is considered 'Done' until its ADR is 'Accepted'.
  • Traceability over Abstraction: Every architectural insight must be linked to evidence in code or intent.
  • Governance as Participation: Architecture governance must be a participant in delivery, not an external checkpoint.
  • Human-in-the-Loop Authority: Automation generates intelligence; humans authorize the evolution.

docs.title: Emergent Heuristics
docs.text: velocity_calibration: When the proposal-to-acceptance ratio exceeds 5:1, new feature development must halt for a Governance Triage cycle. intent_priority: No Architecture Work (AW) should reach 'Active' status without a direct link to a Business Intent signal.

docs.title: Strategic Vision
docs.text:
• What we are building: A deterministic, self-enforcing architecture governance substrate that transitions from template-ready scaffolding to an operational, CI-validated pipeline.
  • Why it exists: To establish a single source of truth for architectural evolution, prevent structural drift, and align with automated alignment validation across the CAS ecosystem.

docs.title: Project Context
docs.text: primary_context: ideas-to-life-cas aw_count: 4 alignment_findings: 2

docs.title: Current State Alignment
docs.text: Partially aligned. Foundation is structurally mature with comprehensive templates and operational runbooks. However, governance execution is absent, resulting in missing ADRs, work logs, and execution history. Pattern coverage is low, and drift detection is currently impossible without CI validation.

docs.title: Focus Areas / Roadmap
docs.text:
• Execute initial discovery flow to generate first Architecture Work and Alignment Report.
  • Create initial ADR for core orchestration pipeline to establish decision baseline.
  • Validate and populate knowledge layer templates with project-specific patterns and rules.
  • Implement automated CI validation for CAS skill enforcement to enable drift detection and continuous alignment.

docs.title: Gaps & Tensions (Risks)
docs.text:
• Absence of populated governance artifacts (ADRs, AW logs) prevents baseline establishment.
  • Lack of execution history makes architectural drift undetectable.
  • Low pattern coverage limits the governance substrate's ability to enforce constraints effectively.
  • Transition from template-ready design to operational execution requires immediate bootstrap actions.

docs.title: Principles
docs.text:
• Governance precedes scaling: Templates must be populated and validated before system-wide adoption.
  • Deterministic derivation: All architectural artifacts must conform to strict schema contracts to enable automation.
  • Enforcement over documentation: CI validation must actively prevent drift rather than passively record it.
  • Single source of truth: The CAS pipeline serves as the authoritative record for all architectural evolution.

docs.title: Emergent Heuristics
docs.text: bootstrap_strategy: Start with high-impact, low-complexity discovery and ADR creation to generate initial governance evidence. pattern_population: Tailor knowledge layer templates to actual project constraints before attempting to scale governance workflows. drift_prevention: Automate skill enforcement early to establish execution history and enable future alignment validation. evolution_path: Move from template-ready -> populated -> enforced -> automated continuous lifecycle.

docs.title: Strategic Vision
docs.text:
• What we are building: An Architecture Intelligence Platform that bridges the gap between business intent, architectural decisions, and code implementation through continuous reasoning and governance loops.
  • Why it exists: To eliminate architectural drift, automate governance participation, and provide real-time traceability from strategic objectives to runtime behavior.

docs.title: Project Context
docs.text: primary_context: ideas-to-life-cas aw_count: 14 alignment_findings: 2

docs.title: Current State Alignment
docs.text: The system is transitioning from a decision-driven model to an intelligence platform. Current alignment is hampered by a 'Decision Persistence Gap' where foundational logic (Orchestration) lacks formal ADRs, and a 'Lifecycle Inconsistency' where AEM stages do not strictly align with AW decision states.

docs.title: Focus Areas / Roadmap
docs.text:
1. Stabilize Baseline: Formalize and accept the ADR for the Core Orchestration Pipeline.
    1. Reconcile Governance: Synchronize AEM Evolution Map stages with authoritative Architecture Work Registry states.
    1. Establish Detection: Prioritize and implement automated CI validation for architectural drift (aw-005).
    1. Operationalize Ops: Define and execute initial ReasoningOps and GovernanceOps loops.
    1. Intent Integration: Develop the ingestion framework for business intent signals.

docs.title: Gaps & Tensions (Risks)
docs.text:
• Decision Persistence Gap: Core Orchestration Pipeline is operational but lacks a finalized, persisted ADR.
  • Observability Gap: Strategic intent for automated drift detection is documented but currently unimplemented in CI/CD.
  • Single Source of Truth Violation: Discrepancies between Architecture Work Registry statuses and AEM summary views.
  • Proposal Saturation: High volume of proposed items (13) relative to accepted decisions (1) indicates a bottleneck in governance throughput.

docs.title: Principles
docs.text:
• Decisions are Central: All architectural evolution must be anchored in persisted, structured ADRs.
  • Traceability over Abstraction: Every architectural insight must be linked to verifiable evidence from code, work, or intent.
  • Governance as Participation: Governance is an embedded operational activity, not an external review event.
  • Extend, Don't Rewrite: Build on existing CAS primitives (Patterns, ADR, AEM, AID).
  • Human Authority: Automation participates and detects, but architects interpret and decide.

docs.title: Emergent Heuristics
docs.text: Detection_Precedes_Evolution: Implementation of drift detection mechanisms must precede complex system evolution to prevent compounding architectural debt. Registry_Authority: The Architecture Work Registry is the only valid source for planning; the AEM is a derived, point-in-time summary. Intent_Driven_Refactoring: Refactoring efforts should be prioritized based on their alignment with business intent signals rather than technical debt alone.

docs.title: Strategic Vision
docs.text:
• What we are building: An Architecture Intelligence Platform that bridges the gap between business intent, architectural decisions, and code implementation.
  • Why it exists: To transform architecture governance from a periodic review activity into a continuous, operationalized capability that prevents structural drift and ensures end-to-end traceability.

docs.title: Project Context
docs.text: primary_context: ideas-to-life-cas aw_count: 14 alignment_findings: 2

docs.title: Current State Alignment
docs.text: The system is currently 'partially aligned'. While the vision is clear, there is a significant governance bottleneck (13 proposed items vs 1 accepted) and a critical provenance gap regarding the Core Orchestration Pipeline.

docs.title: Focus Areas / Roadmap
docs.text:
• Immediate: Stabilize the baseline by accepting the Core Orchestration Pipeline ADR and reconciling the Architecture Work Registry.
  • Short-term: Implement automated CI validation for skill enforcement and align AEM generation logic with decision status rules.
  • Medium-term: Operationalize ReasoningOps and GovernanceOps loops to handle the proposal backlog.
  • Long-term: Integrate business intent signals and deploy the Code Intelligence layer for automated drift detection.

docs.title: Gaps & Tensions (Risks)
docs.text:
• Governance Throughput: High volume of proposed architecture work items indicates a bottleneck in the 'Architect Decides' phase.
  • Provenance Gap: The Core Orchestration Pipeline is operational but lacks a finalized, accepted ADR, creating a foundation of 'implicit' architecture.
  • Registry Authority Drift: Inconsistencies between the authoritative Architecture Work Registry and derived AEM summary views.
  • Lifecycle Misalignment: Evolution Map stages (NEXT/DONE) are being assigned to items before their underlying decisions are formally accepted.

docs.title: Principles
docs.text:
• Registry Authority: The Architecture Work Registry is the single authoritative source for all planning and state.
  • Decision-Driven Evolution: No system evolution occurs without a corresponding accepted decision (ADR).
  • Operational Participation: Governance must participate in the delivery flow (Prevent -> Detect -> Feedback).
  • Traceability over Abstraction: Every architectural insight must be linked to business, code, or decision evidence.
  • Human-in-the-Loop: Automation proposes and detects, but architects remain the authoritative deciders.

docs.title: Emergent Heuristics
docs.text: Governance_Saturation_Alert: If the ratio of proposed to accepted items exceeds 5:1, prioritize a governance review session over new discovery work. Drift_Correction_Priority: Reconciling the authoritative registry takes precedence over generating new architectural intent when drift is detected. Provenance_Enforcement: An item cannot move to 'NEXT' in the evolution map until its ADR status is 'accepted'.

docs.text:

  • Why it exists: To transform architecture governance from a periodic review activity into a continuous, operationalized capability that prevents drift and ensures structural integrity.

docs.title: Focus Areas / Roadmap
docs.text:

  • Phase 3: Automated Guardrails & Continuous Governance (Medium-term) - Deploy CI pipeline validation for CAS skill enforcement, operationalize Detect-Analyse-Execute governance loop, establish ReasoningOps/GovernanceOps feedback mechanisms.
  • Phase 4: Ecosystem & Knowledge Graph Expansion (Future) - Integrate with enterprise architecture tools, build cross-project knowledge graph, develop interactive Architecture Intelligence Maps (AIM).

docs.title: Gaps & Tensions (Risks)
docs.text:
• Governance velocity bottleneck: High proposal backlog (22 items) vs low acceptance rate (1 item) stalls architectural evolution.

  • Decision provenance void: Missing persisted ADR for the core orchestration pipeline creates architectural single-point-of-failure risk.
  • Strategic alignment absence: No business intent signal ingestion or code intelligence layer exists to detect drift or map intent to implementation.
  • Manual enforcement reliance: CI validation for CAS skills is absent, leaving guardrails to contributor discipline rather than automated pipelines.

docs.title: Gaps & Tensions (Risks)
docs.text:
• Governance Velocity Bottleneck: Implementation is progressing at 15x the rate of formal decision recording.
  • Strategic Intent Gap: Missing formal connection between high-level business objectives and technical patterns.
  • Persistence Lag: Implementation 'DONE' states lack corresponding 'ACCEPTED' ADRs.
  • Stale Decision Status: Critical governance standards (e.g., Diagram Governance) remain in pending states while being actively applied.

docs.title: Principles
docs.text:
• {'name': 'Decisions are Central', 'statement': 'All system capabilities must integrate with or derive from an Architecture Decision Record (ADR).'}
  • {'name': 'Intent-First', 'statement': 'Architectural changes must be traceable back to business intent before promotion.'}
  • {'name': 'Governance as Participation', 'statement': 'Governance should be an active participant in the delivery workflow, not a periodic review event.'}
  • {'name': 'Traceability over Abstraction', 'statement': 'Every architectural insight or pattern must be linked to concrete implementation evidence.'}

docs.title: Emergent Heuristics
docs.text: governance_velocity_threshold: Maintain a maximum 5:1 ratio of proposed items to accepted decisions to prevent persistence lag. evidence_calibration: A 'DONE' state in the AEM without an 'ACCEPTED' ADR constitutes a critical governance failure.

docs.title: Strategic Vision
docs.text:
• What we are building: An integrated Architecture Intelligence Platform enabling a continuous loop between Business Intent, Architecture State, and Code Implementation.
  • Why it exists: To eliminate the 15:1 governance velocity bottleneck by embedding governance directly into operational delivery via ReasoningOps.

docs.title: Project Context
docs.text: primary_context: ideas-to-life-cas aw_count: 29 alignment_findings: 3

docs.title: Current State Alignment
docs.text: The system is in a state of 'Critical Persistence Lag'. Implementation of core primitives (Work Registry, Governance Portal) has outpaced formal decision recording, creating a 22:1 proposal-to-acceptance imbalance.

docs.title: Focus Areas / Roadmap
docs.text:
• Phase 1: Governance Reconciliation (Backfill ADRs for Work Registry and Portal).
  • Phase 2: Governance Triage (Process or prune the 22-item backlog).
  • Phase 3: Business Intent Schema Definition (Establish structural requirements for BRD/Context ingestion).
  • Phase 4: Knowledge Graph Implementation (Transition from document-based to graph-based traceability).
  • Phase 5: Automated Drift Remediation (Implement 'Detect-Analyse-Execute' loop).

docs.title: Gaps & Tensions (Risks)
docs.text:
• Governance Velocity Breach: Intelligence generation speed exceeds human review capacity.
  • Implicit Core Implementation: Critical primitives are 'DONE' in implementation but 'PROPOSED' in governance.
  • Business Intent Disconnect: High strategic priority for intent integration but zero implementation evidence in current AEM.
  • Drift Persistence: Formal ADRs are lagging behind the actual system state, leading to 'un-governed' stability.

docs.title: Principles
docs.text:
• Decisions are Central: No capability is considered 'Done' until its ADR is 'Accepted'.
  • Traceability over Abstraction: Every architectural insight must be linked to evidence in code or intent.
  • Governance as Participation: Architecture governance must be a participant in delivery, not an external checkpoint.
  • Human-in-the-Loop Authority: Automation generates intelligence; humans authorize the evolution.

docs.title: Emergent Heuristics
docs.text: velocity_calibration: When the proposal-to-acceptance ratio exceeds 5:1, new feature development must halt for a Governance Triage cycle. intent_priority: No Architecture Work (AW) should reach 'Active' status without a direct link to a Business Intent signal.

docs.title: Institutional Patterns
docs.text:
Reusable architectural patterns approved by the Architecture Forum.

Pattern Classification Maturity Confidence Decoupled Reasoning and Orchestration N/A N/A N/A

docs.title: Intent
docs.text: Separate orchestration, reasoning, and knowledge access responsibilities through explicit contracts to enable implementation-agnostic workflow execution.

docs.title: Context
docs.text: The CAS framework requires a clear separation between the orchestration layer (Runner), reasoning logic (Workflows), and data access layer (Knowledge Providers). This separation is essential for systems adopting the CAS workflow model to ensure modularity and portability across different execution environments.

docs.title: Agentic Profile
docs.text: The CAS Runner orchestrates execution and validates compliance, while Workflows encapsulate reasoning logic and Knowledge Providers supply context. Agents must adhere to prompt-based contracts to maintain decoupling.

docs.title: Forces
docs.text: The pattern balances portability, modularity, and governance requirements through explicit contracts.

docs.title: Drivers
docs.text:
• Portability across execution environments
  • Modular evolution of reasoning capabilities
  • Implementation-agnostic workflow design

docs.title: Constraints
docs.text:
• Strict separation of reasoning logic from orchestration runners
  • Prompt-based contracts for data access abstraction

docs.title: Tradeoffs
docs.text:
• Additional contract management overhead
  • Potential latency from abstraction layers

docs.title: Solution
docs.text: Implement a decoupled execution model where the CAS Runner handles orchestration and validation, Workflows contain pure reasoning logic, and Knowledge Providers abstract data access. Communication between layers is enforced via prompt-based contracts, ensuring that reasoning logic remains independent of storage and execution infrastructure.

docs.title: Operationalisation
docs.text: Adopting this pattern requires restructuring workflow implementations to enforce layer separation and integrating automated validation into the CI/CD pipeline.

docs.title: Workflow Implications
docs.text:
• Refactor existing workflows to isolate reasoning logic from orchestration calls
  • Define and version prompt-based contracts for Knowledge Provider interactions
  • Update onboarding specifications to reference the decoupled execution model

docs.title: Automation Opportunities
docs.text:
• Implement CI/CD validation to detect violations of the decoupled execution model
  • Automate compliance checks through architecture alignment reviews
  • Generate runtime-native AW artifacts to monitor pattern adherence

docs.title: Implementation Signals
docs.text: Signals include the presence of distinct Runner, Workflow, and Knowledge Provider components, explicit prompt-based contracts between layers, and CI/CD guardrails enforcing decoupling.

docs.title: Evidence
docs.text: High confidence based on recurring architecture work, current state support, and cross-project applicability.

docs.title: Signals
docs.text:
• recurring_architecture_work
  • current_architecture_state_support
  • cross_project_applicability

docs.title: Supporting Evidence
docs.text:
• aw-ideas-to-life-cas-20260605-04
  • aw-ideas-to-life-cas-20260605-03

docs.title: Confidence Basis
docs.text: High confidence due to successful implementation in the ideas-to-life-cas project, strong evidence from multiple architecture work items, and alignment with the CAS framework's foundational execution model.

docs.title: Consequences
docs.text: Adopting this pattern increases modularity and portability but requires strict adherence to contract definitions and governance validation.

docs.title: Benefits
docs.text:
• Increased modularity and portability of reasoning logic
  • Implementation-agnostic execution across different environments
  • Clear separation of concerns simplifies maintenance and evolution

docs.title: Risks
docs.text:
• Contract maintenance overhead if prompt interfaces change frequently
  • Potential performance overhead from abstraction layers

docs.title: Governance Implications
docs.text:
• Mandatory compliance for all CAS-based workflows
  • Requires structured YAML ADRs for future execution model changes
  • CI/CD validation and architecture alignment reviews are required for enforcement

docs.title: Reuse Notes
docs.text: Applies to all CAS-based workflow implementations requiring separation between reasoning logic and orchestration runners. Reference this pattern from future workflow onboarding specifications.

docs.title: Confidence
docs.text:
High confidence in the pattern's effectiveness and maturity.

Level: high

docs.title: Rationale
docs.text: The pattern defines the foundational execution model for the CAS framework, is implementation-agnostic, and has been validated through recurring architecture work and cross-project applicability.

docs.title: Current Architecture State
docs.text:
Latest State

Showing latest version: current-architecture-state (2026-06-22)

docs.title: Executive Summary
docs.text: The ideas-to-life-cas project is transitioning from a template-ready documentation model to an operational Architecture Intelligence Platform. While foundational structures (CAS Runner, Knowledge Provider, and Governance Portal) are operational, the system is currently 'partially aligned' due to significant backlog pressure (22 unprogressed proposals) and a persistence lag where implementation progress has outpaced formal decision recording (ADRs).

docs.title: Architecture Evolution Summary
docs.text: Observable evolution includes the institutionalization of the Architecture Work Registry as the authoritative backlog, the implementation of a published Governance Portal (MkDocs/GitHub Pages), and the abstraction of the Knowledge Provider to decouple workflows from storage. The system has moved from manual execution to semi-automated orchestration via the CAS Runner.

docs.title: Architecture Evolution
docs.text: The project is on a trajectory toward 'Continuous Architecture Governance'. Maturity in structural contracts and operational guidance is high, but governance execution maturity is currently bottlenecked by a 15:1 governance velocity ratio, necessitating a shift toward automated Detect-Analyse-Execute loops.

docs.title: Capability Landscape
docs.text: Operational capabilities include the CAS Runner, Knowledge Provider Abstraction, and Governance Portal. Active capabilities include the Architecture Work Registry and Alignment Engine. Emerging capabilities include Business Intent Integration, Code Intelligence, and the Architecture Knowledge Graph.

docs.title: Capability Dependencies
docs.text: The Architecture Work Registry serves as the source for AEM and AID generation. The Knowledge Provider Abstraction is a prerequisite for the Architecture Knowledge Graph. The Alignment Engine depends on the Rule Context and the Workflow Execution Model.

docs.title: Initiative Landscape
docs.text: Governance Foundation is active and stabilizing. Architecture Work Registry is active with its authoritative status accepted. Operational Intelligence and ReasoningOps are in the proposed/planning phase, focusing on cross-project lineage and continuous evaluation loops.

docs.title: Governance State
docs.text: Governance is currently 'partially aligned'. The primary gap is 'Backlog Pressure' (22 proposals vs 6 completed items). A 'Persistence Lag' exists where core components like the Workflow Execution Model are implemented but lack formal ADR acceptance. Governance is shifting from periodic review to a participation model embedded in delivery.

docs.title: Architecture State
docs.text: The architecture is defined by a standardized Workflow Execution Model (Runner/Workflow/KnowledgeProvider). The system has adopted a 'Rules as Authority' model over separate policies. The Architecture Work Registry is now the single source of truth for architectural change, resolving previous issues with planning state loss during regeneration.

docs.title: Intelligence State
docs.text: Intelligence is currently focused on drift detection via the Alignment Engine. Future state plans include an Architecture Intelligence Layer for signal detection, pattern discovery, and a Knowledge Graph for multi-hop lineage analysis across the portfolio.

docs.title: Operational State
docs.text: Operational maturity is supported by a live MkDocs portal and a standardized runner. Automated CI validation for CAS skill enforcement is proposed to prevent architectural drift. The current operating model relies on 'Prevent-Detect-Feedback' loops.

docs.title: Architecture State Metrics
docs.text: Total Architecture Work items: 30. Status distribution: 22 Proposed, 6 Done, 1 Accepted, 1 Dummy. Alignment findings: 3 High, 3 Medium. Governance velocity ratio: 15:1.

docs.title: Strategic Gaps
docs.text: 1. Persistence Lag: Implementation (DONE) outpaces formal ADRs. 2. Intent Definition Gap: Lack of formal Business Intent schema prevents full traceability. 3. Backlog Pressure: System generates proposals faster than human validation capacity. 4. Derivation Mismatch: AEM stages are implementation-driven rather than decision-driven.

docs.title: Observed State
docs.text: The system exhibits a robust structural foundation with active alignment reporting and a published governance surface, but implementation reality is frequently ahead of governed decision records.

docs.title: Interpreted State
docs.text: The project has successfully established 'Governance Foundation v1' but is struggling with the transition to 'Continuous Governance' due to the manual overhead of processing high-volume architectural signals.

docs.title: Hypothesised State
docs.text: Implementing a 'Governance Action Framework' and automated remediation signals within the Alignment Engine will be required to reduce the 15:1 velocity bottleneck and clear the proposal backlog.

docs.title: Confidence Assessment
docs.text: High confidence in the structural and foundational state based on comprehensive baseline and AEM data. Medium confidence in implementation alignment due to the identified persistence lag and unpopulated project-specific patterns.

docs.title: Evidence Sources
docs.text: Synthesis of Architecture Evolution Map (v1), Architecture Intent Document (2026-06-17), Alignment Report (2026-06-17), CAS Future Vision, and the Architecture Work Registry (30 items).

docs.title: Excessive Backlog Pressure
docs.text:
• Confidence: HIGH
  • Evidence: alignment-report-ideas-to-life-cas-2026-06-17-1, aem-ideas-to-life-cas-2026-06-17-01
  • Rationale: 22 proposed items remain unprogressed, indicating the system generates intelligence faster than the architect can validate it.

docs.text:
• Confidence: HIGH
  • Evidence: alignment-report-ideas-to-life-cas-2026-06-17-1, aw-ideas-to-life-cas-20260605-04
  • Rationale: Core capabilities like the Workflow Execution Model are marked DONE but lack corresponding 'accepted' ADRs.

docs.title: Strategic Identity: Architecture Intelligence Platform
docs.text:
• Confidence: HIGH
  • Evidence: cas-future-vision, aid-ideas-to-life-cas-2026-06-17
  • Rationale: The vision and intent documents consistently prioritize connecting Business Intent to Code Reality via an intelligence layer.

docs.title: Current Architecture State
docs.text: Generated intelligence — derived from authoritative governance artifacts. Not a source of truth.

docs.title: Executive Summary
docs.text:
CAS is in a template-stable, operationally governed, and partially aligned state. The project has moved decisively out of the 2024-05-20 "template-ready, no live governance evidence" baseline and into an actively governed baseline defined by:

  • A persistent Architecture Work Registry that now holds authoritative planning state.
  • A standardised CAS Workflow Execution Model (Runner orchestrates, Workflow reasons, Knowledge Provider supplies context) — accepted, operational.
  • Runtime-native Architecture Work generation (conversation-to-aw, discovery-to-aw, vision-to-aw) — operational.
  • A continuously produced Architecture Evolution Map (AEM), Architecture Intent Document (AID), and Alignment Report chain — six consecutive AEM versions, five AID versions, five alignment reports.
  • 21 active architecture lineage capabilities (reasoning, governance, steering, observability, semantic contracts, provider abstraction).
  • 11 ADRs persisted in the global knowledge layer — 5 accepted, 5 pending, 1 rejected.

The system is bottlenecked by a governance velocity gap (30 proposed Architecture Work items vs 2 accepted in the current AEM) and a structured-decision schema non-compliance (legacy ADRs remain narrative; rule-decision-schema-must-be-structured is in READY, not yet accepted). The Core Orchestration Pipeline still lacks its foundational ADR.

Alignment status is partially_aligned with 0 critical, 3 high, and 3 medium findings. The dominant theme is governance backlog processing, not architectural fragility.

docs.title: Governance State
docs.text:
CAS governance is in transition from a template-ready, manually-triggered model to an operationally governed, rules-driven model.

  • Authoritative artifacts: ADRs (11), Architecture Work (41 instances in work/, of which 30 are proposed), AEM (6 daily versions), AID (5 versions), Alignment Reports (5 versions).
  • Rule set: 19 global rules active in the alignment engine; rule conflicts and prompt-rule audit reports produced (5 conflict reports, 4 prompt audits).
  • Lifecycle coverage: All governance artifacts carry schema-validated frontmatter; rule precedence hierarchy enforced (rule-precedence-hierarchy); no project-scoped rules exist.
  • Provenance: Architecture Work is now the planning source of truth; AEM and AID are derived.
  • Human-in-the-loop: Enforced by rule-system-proposes-architect-decides and rule-decision-status-drives-stage.

Governance maturity: Emerging → Active. Structure is in place; velocity is constrained by the proposal backlog and a schema-compliance gap.

docs.title: Architecture State
docs.text:
Major architectural structures, models, and patterns currently in operation:

  • Workflow Execution Model — Runner / Workflow / Knowledge Provider separation, with ReasoningExecutionContract mediating prompt-to-LLM interaction.
  • Architecture Knowledge Layer — knowledge/global/ and knowledge/projects/<project>/ partitioning, with templates, rules, patterns, decisions, maps, intent, alignment, and ingestion directories.
  • Architecture Work Registry — Persistent, schema-validated planning layer.
  • AEM / AID / Alignment chain — Generated governance dashboard artifacts.
  • Rules Context Loader — Loads global and project rules into the reasoning context at execution time.
  • Steering Projection — Git delta → knowledge artifact pipeline (steering-projection-flow).
  • LLM Provider Abstraction — Provider-agnostic LLM boundary with structured output.
  • Pattern Catalogue — 17 patterns persisted as global architecture knowledge.
  • Architecture Lineage — Module-level capability tracking via capabilities/lineage.yaml.

Established patterns (representative): architecture-workflow-loop, build-time-governance, contract-first-architecture, deterministic-core-selective-augmentation, heuristic-driven-arbitration, llm-provider-abstraction-boundary, orchestrator-managed-pipeline, prompt-as-code-separation, prompt-schema-contracts-as-architecture, structured-output-via-schema, typed-execution-context.

docs.title: Intelligence State
docs.text:
• Available intelligence capabilities:
  • Architecture Work generation (conversation / discovery / vision / alignment producers).
  • AEM regeneration from authoritative artifacts.
  • AID synthesis aligned with system state.
  • Alignment analysis applying the full rule context.
  • Rule audits (prompt-rules-audit-*) and rule conflict reports (rule-conflicts-*).
  • Architecture lineage tracking (21 active capabilities).
  • Heuristic governance health model (Dashboard ADR-030).
  • Planned / not yet operational:
  • Code intelligence (structural analysis, drift detection).
  • Business intent signal extraction and traceability.
  • Interactive Architecture Intelligence Map (AIM).
  • Knowledge Graph layer for cross-project queries.

docs.title: Operational State
docs.text:
• Operational governance capabilities: Rules-driven alignment analysis, daily AEM regeneration, daily AID synthesis, daily steering projection, governance audits, schema-validated frontmatter across artifacts.
  • Automation capabilities: Runtime-native Architecture Work generation (operational), CI workflows cas-managed-steering-sync.yml and cas-portal-publish.yml (active), cas run <flow> orchestration CLI (active), lineage tracking (automatic).
  • Operational governance maturity: Emerging. Automation exists for the core loop (Runner + AEM + AID + Alignment + AW); enforcement automation (CI validation enforcing CAS skill compliance) is proposed but not yet implemented.
  • Trust calibration: Heuristic governance health model is the approved Phase 1 mechanism (ADR-030), and the dashboard is read-only intelligence.

docs.title: Strategic Gaps
docs.text:
Evidence-backed gaps between current and intended architecture:

  1. YAML ADR migration is incomplete. rule-decision-schema-must-be-structured is active; 11 ADRs persist as narrative Markdown. (Alignment HIGH; aw-2026-06-06-01 proposed; AEM READY.)
  2. Core Orchestration Pipeline ADR is missing. Foundational decision provenance for the orchestration pipeline is absent. (Alignment HIGH; aw-2026-06-06-02 proposed; AEM READY.)
  3. Governance velocity bottleneck. 30 Architecture Work proposals vs 2 accepted in the current AEM (proposed:accepted ratio of 15:1, exceeding the 5:1 heuristic threshold from the AID). (Alignment HIGH; AID risk.)
  4. Automated CI validation for CAS skills is absent. No enforced guardrail at PR time. (Alignment MEDIUM; AID risk.)
  5. Business Intent knowledge model is absent. Traceability from business requirement → ADR → pattern → code is not yet possible. (Alignment MEDIUM; aw-2026-05-31-006 proposed.)
  6. Code Intelligence is absent. No structural code analysis, no automated drift detection. (AID long-term roadmap; aw-2026-05-31-007 proposed.)
  7. AID/AEM business_traceability coverage is low. (AID coverage field.)
  8. Project-scoped rules layer is empty. All rules are global; knowledge/projects/*/rules/ is unused. (Rule conflict report note.)

docs.title: Strong evidence
docs.text:
• Workflow Execution Model adoption (acceptance + completion AW + AEM DONE).
  • Architecture Work Registry as authoritative backlog (acceptance + lineage).
  • AEM / AID / Alignment daily regeneration (six/five/five consecutive artifacts).
  • Architecture Work runtime generation (completed AW + AEM DONE).
  • 21 active lineage capabilities.
  • Dashboard layered architecture (ADR-030 accepted).
  • Rule precedence hierarchy and human-in-the-loop governance (active rules).

docs.title: Uncertain areas
docs.text:
• Effective governance throughput once the 30-item proposal backlog is processed (heuristic only).
  • Live runtime status of the Governance Health Dashboard (ADR accepted, runtime delivery not yet confirmed in AEM).
  • Maturity of LLM gateway integration (ADR pending, no operational evidence).
  • Coverage of the prompt-rule audit cleanup (issues flagged; no completion artifact in evidence).

docs.title: Missing evidence
docs.text:
• No Architecture Work signal-layer evidence for emergent patterns.
  • No project-scoped rules evidence (knowledge/projects/*/rules/ is empty).
  • No active measurement of drift between AID and code (no Code Intelligence yet).
  • No measurement of cross-project signals or AEMs (single project in scope).
  • No recorded evidence that the proposed AEM READY actions (initial discovery flow, core orchestration ADR, YAML schema transition) have been moved to APPROVED/DONE within the captured artifacts.

docs.text: Artefact Reference Architecture Evolution Map knowledge/global/maps/aem-2026-06-06.md Architecture Intent Document knowledge/projects/ideas-to-life-cas/intent/aid-2026-06-06.md Alignment Report knowledge/projects/ideas-to-life-cas/alignment/alignment-2026-06-06.md Architecture Work (registry anchor) aw-ideas-to-life-cas-2026-05-31-001, aw-2026-06-06-04, aw-2026-06-06-05, aw-2026-06-06-01, aw-2026-06-06-02 Vision knowledge/projects/ideas-to-life-cas/vision/cas-future-vision.md Baseline knowledge/projects/ideas-to-life-cas/baseline/baseline-assessment-2024-05-20.md ADRs (global) knowledge/global/decisions/adr-*.md (11 records) Capability Lineage knowledge/projects/ideas-to-life-cas/capabilities/lineage.yaml Rule Audit knowledge/global/governance/prompt-rules-audit-2026-04-22-02.md Rule Conflicts knowledge/global/governance/rule-conflicts-ideas-to-life-cas-2026-04-21-01.md Dashboard ADR knowledge/global/decisions/adr-030-dashboard-architecture.md

docs.title: Executive Summary
docs.text: The ideas-to-life-cas project is transitioning from a template-ready documentation model to an operational Architecture Intelligence Platform. While foundational structures (CAS Runner, Knowledge Provider, and Governance Portal) are operational, the system is currently 'partially aligned' due to significant backlog pressure (22 unprogressed proposals) and a persistence lag where implementation progress has outpaced formal decision recording (ADRs).

docs.title: Architecture Evolution Summary
docs.text: Observable evolution includes the institutionalization of the Architecture Work Registry as the authoritative backlog, the implementation of a published Governance Portal (MkDocs/GitHub Pages), and the abstraction of the Knowledge Provider to decouple workflows from storage. The system has moved from manual execution to semi-automated orchestration via the CAS Runner.

docs.title: Architecture Evolution
docs.text: The project is on a trajectory toward 'Continuous Architecture Governance'. Maturity in structural contracts and operational guidance is high, but governance execution maturity is currently bottlenecked by a 15:1 governance velocity ratio, necessitating a shift toward automated Detect-Analyse-Execute loops.

docs.title: Capability Landscape
docs.text: Operational capabilities include the CAS Runner, Knowledge Provider Abstraction, and Governance Portal. Active capabilities include the Architecture Work Registry and Alignment Engine. Emerging capabilities include Business Intent Integration, Code Intelligence, and the Architecture Knowledge Graph.

docs.title: Capability Dependencies
docs.text: The Architecture Work Registry serves as the source for AEM and AID generation. The Knowledge Provider Abstraction is a prerequisite for the Architecture Knowledge Graph. The Alignment Engine depends on the Rule Context and the Workflow Execution Model.

docs.title: Initiative Landscape
docs.text: Governance Foundation is active and stabilizing. Architecture Work Registry is active with its authoritative status accepted. Operational Intelligence and ReasoningOps are in the proposed/planning phase, focusing on cross-project lineage and continuous evaluation loops.

docs.title: Governance State
docs.text: Governance is currently 'partially aligned'. The primary gap is 'Backlog Pressure' (22 proposals vs 6 completed items). A 'Persistence Lag' exists where core components like the Workflow Execution Model are implemented but lack formal ADR acceptance. Governance is shifting from periodic review to a participation model embedded in delivery.

docs.title: Architecture State
docs.text: The architecture is defined by a standardized Workflow Execution Model (Runner/Workflow/KnowledgeProvider). The system has adopted a 'Rules as Authority' model over separate policies. The Architecture Work Registry is now the single source of truth for architectural change, resolving previous issues with planning state loss during regeneration.

docs.title: Intelligence State
docs.text: Intelligence is currently focused on drift detection via the Alignment Engine. Future state plans include an Architecture Intelligence Layer for signal detection, pattern discovery, and a Knowledge Graph for multi-hop lineage analysis across the portfolio.

docs.title: Operational State
docs.text: Operational maturity is supported by a live MkDocs portal and a standardized runner. Automated CI validation for CAS skill enforcement is proposed to prevent architectural drift. The current operating model relies on 'Prevent-Detect-Feedback' loops.

docs.title: Architecture State Metrics
docs.text: Total Architecture Work items: 30. Status distribution: 22 Proposed, 6 Done, 1 Accepted, 1 Dummy. Alignment findings: 3 High, 3 Medium. Governance velocity ratio: 15:1.

docs.title: Strategic Gaps
docs.text: 1. Persistence Lag: Implementation (DONE) outpaces formal ADRs. 2. Intent Definition Gap: Lack of formal Business Intent schema prevents full traceability. 3. Backlog Pressure: System generates proposals faster than human validation capacity. 4. Derivation Mismatch: AEM stages are implementation-driven rather than decision-driven.

docs.title: Observed State
docs.text: The system exhibits a robust structural foundation with active alignment reporting and a published governance surface, but implementation reality is frequently ahead of governed decision records.

docs.title: Interpreted State
docs.text: The project has successfully established 'Governance Foundation v1' but is struggling with the transition to 'Continuous Governance' due to the manual overhead of processing high-volume architectural signals.

docs.title: Hypothesised State
docs.text: Implementing a 'Governance Action Framework' and automated remediation signals within the Alignment Engine will be required to reduce the 15:1 velocity bottleneck and clear the proposal backlog.

docs.title: Confidence Assessment
docs.text: High confidence in the structural and foundational state based on comprehensive baseline and AEM data. Medium confidence in implementation alignment due to the identified persistence lag and unpopulated project-specific patterns.

docs.title: Evidence Sources
docs.text: Synthesis of Architecture Evolution Map (v1), Architecture Intent Document (2026-06-17), Alignment Report (2026-06-17), CAS Future Vision, and the Architecture Work Registry (30 items).

docs.title: Excessive Backlog Pressure
docs.text:
• Confidence: HIGH
  • Evidence: alignment-report-ideas-to-life-cas-2026-06-17-1, aem-ideas-to-life-cas-2026-06-17-01
  • Rationale: 22 proposed items remain unprogressed, indicating the system generates intelligence faster than the architect can validate it.

docs.title: Persistence Lag in Decision Records
docs.text:
• Confidence: HIGH
  • Evidence: alignment-report-ideas-to-life-cas-2026-06-17-1, aw-ideas-to-life-cas-20260605-04
  • Rationale: Core capabilities like the Workflow Execution Model are marked DONE but lack corresponding 'accepted' ADRs.

docs.title: Strategic Identity: Architecture Intelligence Platform
docs.text:
• Confidence: HIGH
  • Evidence: cas-future-vision, aid-ideas-to-life-cas-2026-06-17
  • Rationale: The vision and intent documents consistently prioritize connecting Business Intent to Code Reality via an intelligence layer.

docs.title: Vision & Strategy
docs.text:
Latest State

Showing latest version: cas-future-vision (N/A)

docs.title: 1. Framing
docs.text:
CAS has reached a stable baseline as a decision-driven architecture system:

Code / Conversations → Architecture Work (Aw) → Patterns → Decisions (ADR) → AEM → AID

The next evolution is to transform CAS into an Architecture Intelligence Platform that connects:

Business Intent ↔ Architecture ↔ Code ↔ Decisions

docs.title: Current Scope (Solved)
docs.text:
• Extract patterns from code and learnings
  • Govern architecture via ADRs
  • Maintain consistent architecture state (AEM)
  • Generate architectural intent (AID)

docs.title: Target Scope (Next)
docs.text:
CAS should answer:

- How to connect business intent to architecture and implementation?
- How to stop bad structure from being created in the first place, and catch what still slips through?
- How to continuously align target architecture (AID) with actual implementation (Code) and system state (AEM)?
- Why was this system built this way?
- Does the implementation match the intended architecture?
- How does business intent map to architecture decisions?
- What is the impact of a change across the system and portfolio?

docs.title: 3.1 Business Layer Integration
docs.text: Goal: Connect business artefacts to architecture and implementation.

docs.title: Inputs
docs.text:
• Business Requirements Documents (BRD)
  • Business Cases
  • Portfolio / initiative definitions
  • Strategic objectives

docs.title: Capabilities
docs.text:
• Extract intent signals from business artefacts
  • Link business intent → patterns → decisions
  • Trace:
Business Requirement → ADR → Pattern → Code

docs.title: Example Use Case
docs.text: • "Which architectural decisions support this business capability?"

docs.title: 3.2 Code Intelligence Layer
docs.text: Goal: Move from pattern extraction to full code understanding.

docs.title: Capabilities
docs.text:
• Structural analysis of codebases
  • Identify architectural boundaries, modules, and dependencies
  • Map code → patterns → architecture
  • Detect deviations from intended architecture (AID)
  • Prevent (stop) bad structure from being created in the first place, and catch (detect & correct) what still slips through

docs.title: Example Use Cases
docs.text:
• "Explain how this system is structured"
  • "Where does the implementation violate the architecture?"
  • "Implmenent a new feature or refactor with confidence by understanding the architectural context"

docs.title: 3.3 Architecture Alignment
docs.text: Goal: Continuously align intent vs reality.

docs.title: Capabilities
docs.text:
• Compare:
AID (intent) vs Code (implementation)
AID vs Solution Architecture Documents
AID vs AEM (state)
  • Detect gaps:
  • Missing components
  • Incorrect boundaries
  • Drift over time

docs.title: Example Use Case
docs.text: • "Show the gap between target architecture and current implementation"

docs.title: 3.4 ReasoningOps & GovernanceOps
docs.text: Goal: Transform architecture governance from periodic review activities into continuously participating operational capabilities.

docs.title: ReasoningOps
docs.text: ReasoningOps enables architecture intelligence generation through structured operational reasoning loops.

docs.title: Capabilities
docs.text:
• Convert conversations, implementation activity, architecture work, and operational signals into architectural intelligence
  • Continuously evaluate alignment between intent, implementation, patterns, decisions, and system state
  • Generate architecture evolution recommendations
  • Surface governance drift, capability evolution, and emerging patterns
  • Support architecture participation throughout delivery rather than only during review events

docs.title: Example Use Cases
docs.text:
• "What architectural tensions are emerging across recent implementation activity?"
  • "Which governance signals require architectural attention?"
  • "What architecture work should be prioritised next?"

docs.title: GovernanceOps
docs.text: GovernanceOps operationalises governance as a lightweight, continuously active participation model embedded within delivery workflows.

docs.title: Capabilities
docs.text:
• Governance-aware workflow participation
  • Continuous validation and trust calibration
  • Governance signal lifecycle management
  • Pattern evolution and governance refinement
  • Operational intelligence integration into architectural decision-making
  • Execution-driven governance evolution

docs.title: Example Use Cases
docs.text:
• "How is governance evolving based on operational execution?"
  • "Which validated implementation patterns should become governed architectural patterns?"
  • "Where is governance drift emerging across workflows?"

docs.title: Operational Model
docs.text:
ReasoningOps and GovernanceOps operate through:

Prevent → Detect → Feedback

Where:

Prevent  = architecture operationalisation
Detect   = operational intelligence and alignment evaluation
Feedback = governance evolution and pattern refinement

The objective is not centralized governance control.

The objective is governance participation embedded directly into operational execution.

docs.title: 3.5 External Ecosystem Integration
docs.text: Goal: Position CAS within enterprise architecture ecosystems.

docs.title: Integrations
docs.text:
• EAM tools (Enterprise Architecture Management)
  • Knowledge platforms (Confluence, Notion)
  • Source control systems
  • CI/CD pipelines (Automate Everything)

docs.title: Capabilities
docs.text:
• Ingest external architecture artefacts
  • Synchronise decisions across systems
  • Export CAS knowledge to enterprise tools

docs.title: Example Use Case
docs.text: • "Push validated patterns into enterprise architecture repository"

docs.title: 3.6 Visual & Interaction Layer (AIM)
docs.text: Goal: Make architecture explorable and interactive.

docs.title: Capabilities
docs.text:
• Interactive Architecture Intelligence Maps (AIM)
  • Decision flow visualisation
  • Pattern relationships graph
  • Timeline of architectural evolution

docs.title: Example Use Case
docs.text: • "Visualise how this architecture evolved over time"

docs.title: 4.1 Traceability Engine
docs.text:
Enable end-to-end traceability:

Business → Decision → Pattern → Code → Runtime Behaviour

docs.title: 4.2 Fit / Gap Analysis Engine
docs.text:
• Automated comparison between:
  • AID vs code
  • AID vs external architecture docs
  • Outputs structured gaps and recommendations

docs.title: 4.3 Knowledge Graph Layer (future)
docs.text:
• Represent CAS knowledge as a graph
  • Enable complex queries:
"Find all decisions impacting payment architecture across projects"

docs.title: 4.4 Plugin / Ingestion Framework
docs.text:
• Standard interface for new inputs:
  • BRD parser
  • Code analyzer
  • External system connectors

docs.title: Scenario 1: Business → Architecture
docs.text:
Input: New business requirement
→ Extract intent signals
→ Map to existing technology patterns
→ Identify missing patterns
→ Generate proposed ADRs
→ Update AEM + AID

docs.title: Scenario 2: Code → Architecture Understanding
docs.text:
Input: Existing codebase
→ Analyse structure
→ Map to patterns
→ Compare with AID
→ Generate gap report

docs.title: Scenario 3: Continuous Governance
docs.text:
Input: Code change / PR
→ Detect impacted patterns
→ Evaluate against ADRs
→ Flag violations
→ Suggest corrective actions

docs.title: Phase 1 (Completed)
docs.text:
Manual workflow execution - Decision-driven architecture system - ADR as source of truth - AEM + AID generation - Code Agents (claude, gemini, codex -> CAS Skills -> SDD framework (GitHub speckit) -> Code -> Commit

Semi-automated workflow execution

  • CAS Runner CLI - CAS Workflow automation - (conversations to architecture work -> Rules Context -> AEM -> Alignment Engine -> AID)

docs.title: Phase 2 (Current)
docs.text:
Governance Foundation v1

  • Operational Governance Model established
  • Governance Workflow Mapping established
  • Operational Participation Mapping established
  • Governance Foundation v1 stabilised
  • Governance Runbook introduced
  • Lightweight governance automation introduced
  • Governance-aware operational cognition diagrams established

ReasoningOps & GovernanceOps

  • ReasoningOps concepts validated through architecture-work generation, alignment analysis, AEM evolution, and operational intelligence extraction
  • GovernanceOps concepts validated through governance workflows, participation models, terminology governance, CI governance, and execution-driven governance evolution

Operational Delivery Validation

  • Demonstrated execution portability across coding agents through CAS Skills and SDD/TDD operational workflows
  • Validated governance continuity independent of individual coding-agent runtime sessions
  • Reinforced lightweight governance over orchestration complexity as a practical operating model

docs.title: Phase 3 (Future)
docs.text:
• Business intent integration and traceability
  • Enterprise architecture ecosystem integration
  • Multi-system architecture intelligence
  • Operational intelligence expansion
  • Selective governance automation driven by operational need

docs.title: 7. Guiding Principles for Evolution
docs.text:
1. Extend, don’t rewrite

  2. Build on existing CAS primitives (patterns, ADR, AEM, AID)

  3. Keep decisions central

  4. All new capabilities must integrate with ADR

  5. Prefer traceability over abstraction

  6. Every insight should be linked to evidence

  7. Human governance remains authoritative

  8. Automation participates, architects interpret, govern, and decide

  9. Start from real use cases

  10. Validate each capability through actual projects

docs.title: 8. Open Questions (Deliberate Exploration)
docs.text:
• How to represent business intent structurally?
  • What is the minimal model for code understanding?
  • How to align CAS workflows with existing SDD (Spec Design Driven) engineering processes?
  • When should alignment checks be triggered (event-driven vs scheduled)?
  • How to scale across multiple projects without noise?
  • What is the right abstraction level for enterprise integration?

docs.title: 9. Summary
docs.text:
CAS is evolving from:

Architecture extraction system

To:

Architecture intelligence platform

That enables:

Understand → Reason → Decide → Align → Evolve → Govern continuously

This document is a living vision. It will evolve as CAS capabilities are validated through real usage.

docs.title: Architecture Work Registry as Authoritative Architectural Backlog
Architecture Work Registry as Authoritative Architectural Backlog
Decision
docs.text: Establish Architecture Work as the authoritative architectural change backlog for CAS.

docs.title: Rationale

AEM regeneration currently discards manually introduced planning information. Architectural evolution should be persisted in Architecture Work artifacts and summarized by downstream governance artifacts.

Decision Type: adoption

Decision Status: proposed

Architecture Work Registry

Description:

Architecture Work becomes the authoritative source of architectural change while AEM becomes a generated architectural dashboard.

Candidate Signal: Regenerated Artifacts Should Not Own Planning State

Insight:

Information repeatedly lost through artifact regeneration should be persisted in authoritative source artifacts rather than generated views.

Candidate Signal: Governance Artifacts Prefer Derived State

Insight:

Governance outputs should summarize architecture state rather than become the source of truth themselves.
